message: feat: add context-aware TUI help surfaces (br-bb0gt.2)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to check which agents are alive, what they are running, and where work is currently landing.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot agents --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Contacts, Projects, System Health.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen as a triage assistant for anomalies, then validate the underlying evidence in the primary screens.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot analytics --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Dashboard, Tool Metrics, Timeline.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen when you need the Git-backed audit trail rather than the indexed mailbox view.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot navigate 'resource://thread/<thread-id>?project=/abs/path&include_bodies=true' --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Attachments, Reservations, Timeline.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to inspect the live ATC operator snapshot, decision details, and retention reports.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot atc --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: System Health, Dashboard, Analytics.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to inspect attachment provenance, preview payloads, and verify that a file came from the sender you expect.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot attachments --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Messages, Explorer, Archive Browser.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to inspect cross-agent contact policy before assuming two agents are allowed to talk.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot contacts --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Agents, Projects, Explorer.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen for first-pass operational triage before drilling into a narrower surface.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot status --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: System Health, Timeline, Analytics.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen for quick mailbox audits across inbox and outbox directions, ack state, and actor filters.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot inbox --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName> --all --include-bodies`
+
+Related screens: Messages, Threads, Search.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to review inbox traffic, inspect message envelopes, and work through detail-pane follow-ups.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot inbox --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName> --all`
+
+Related screens: Threads, Explorer, Attachments.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to verify project scope, routing, and which repositories are contributing traffic to the server.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot projects --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Agents, Reservations, Contacts.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to audit leases, resolve conflicts, and decide whether a reservation should be renewed, released, or escalated.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot reservations --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName> --conflicts`
+
+Related screens: Agents, Projects, Archive Browser.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen when you need one query surface across messages, threads, agents, and projects.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot search <query> --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Messages, Threads, Explorer.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to confirm the DB, archive, probes, queues, and ATC runtime are healthy before blaming higher layers.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot health --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Dashboard, Tool Metrics, ATC.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen when chronology and reply context matter more than individual message rows.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot thread <thread-id> --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName> --format md`
+
+Related screens: Messages, Search, Explorer.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to reconstruct incidents from event order, correlation links, and commit-adjacent activity.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot timeline --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName> --since <iso8601>`
+
+Related screens: Dashboard, Threads, Analytics.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use this screen to find latency regressions, failure spikes, and uneven tool traffic before they turn into operator pain.
+
+Bindings on this screen are rendered live below from the registry, so the overlay stays correct when keybindings change.
+
+Robot CLI equivalent: `am robot metrics --project /abs/path --agent <AgentName>`
+
+Related screens: Dashboard, System Health, Analytics.

@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Use `F1` or `?` to open context-aware help for the active screen. Use `j`/`k` or the arrow keys to scroll, and press any other key to dismiss the overlay and return to the screen.
+
+Bindings shown in this overlay come from the live keybinding registry, so the help surface stays in sync with the active profile, per-screen bindings, and future keymap changes.
+
+Use the command palette for discoverability, direct jump keys for fast navigation, and `am robot ...` commands when you need the same operational state without launching the TUI.
+
+Related docs: `docs/TUI_V2_CONTRACT.md`, `docs/OPERATOR_COOKBOOK.md`, `docs/OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md`.
